data.content:
### POST /v1/account_links

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/standard-accounts

Call the Account Links API to create a link for a connected account to onboard with. This link redirects the user to the Stripe Connect Onboarding flow to complete their account setup.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/account_links

### Description
This endpoint generates a short-lived URL that enables your connected account to access the Connect Onboarding flow. You must provide the `account` ID, a `refresh_url`, and a `return_url`.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/account_links

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None)

#### Request Body
- **account** (string) - Required - The identifier of the account for which to create an account link.
- **refresh_url** (string) - Required - The URL that your user will be redirected to if the account link expires, or if the user clicks "return to platform" without completing the onboarding process.
- **return_url** (string) - Required - The URL that your user will be redirected to after they complete the onboarding process.
- **type** (string) - Required - The type of account link to create. For onboarding, use `account_onboarding`.
- **collection_options** (object) - Optional - Configuration for how the account link will collect information. For example, `{"fields": "currently_due", "future_requirements": "omit"}`.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "account": "acct_1G2h3j4k5l6m7n8o",
  "refresh_url": "https://example.com/reauth",
  "return_url": "https://example.com/return",
  "type": "account_onboarding"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Upon successful creation, Stripe returns an Account Link object containing a URL.
- **url** (string) - The URL that your user should be redirected to to continue the onboarding process.
- **expires_at** (integer) - The timestamp in seconds at which the account link URL will expire.
- **object** (string) - Value is `account_link`.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "object": "account_link",
  "created": 1678886400,
  "expires_at": 1678887000,
  "url": "https://connect.stripe.com/setup/acct_1G2h3j4k5l6m7n8o/test_url_some_hash"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/accounts

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/standard-accounts

This endpoint allows you to create a new Stripe Connect account. You can specify the type of account, such as 'standard', to enable various Connect functionalities.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/accounts

### Description
This endpoint allows you to create a new Stripe Connect account. You can specify the type of account, such as 'standard', to enable various Connect functionalities.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/accounts

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None)

#### Request Body
- **type** (string) - Required - The type of account to create. Currently, 'standard' is a common value.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "type": "standard"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the created account.
- **object** (string) - Value is "account".
- **type** (string) - The type of the account, e.g., "standard".
- **charges_enabled** (boolean) - Indicates if the account can process charges.
- **payouts_enabled** (boolean) - Indicates if the account can receive payouts.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "acct_example_id",
  "object": "account",
  "type": "standard",
  "charges_enabled": false,
  "payouts_enabled": false
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/account_links

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/standard-accounts

This endpoint enables you to create an account link, which provides a temporary URL for securely onboarding a connected account. The link directs the user through the Connect onboarding flow.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/account_links

### Description
This endpoint enables you to create an account link, which provides a temporary URL for securely onboarding a connected account. The link directs the user through the Connect onboarding flow.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/account_links

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None)

#### Request Body
- **account** (string) - Required - The ID of the connected account for which to create an account link.
- **refresh_url** (string) - Required - The URL to redirect the user to if their session expires or they need to refresh the page.
- **return_url** (string) - Required - The URL to redirect the user to after they have completed the Connect onboarding flow.
- **type** (string) - Required - The type of account link to create. Use 'account_onboarding' for standard onboarding flows.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "account": "{{CONNECTEDACCOUNT_ID}}",
  "refresh_url": "https://example.com/reauth",
  "return_url": "https://example.com/return",
  "type": "account_onboarding"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **object** (string) - Value is "account_link".
- **created** (integer) - The Unix timestamp of when the account link was created.
- **expires_at** (integer) - The Unix timestamp of when the account link URL will expire.
- **url** (string) - The URL that can be used to connect the account. This is a temporary, one-time-use URL.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "object": "account_link",
  "created": 1678886400,
  "expires_at": 1678890000,
  "url": "https://connect.stripe.com/onboarding/acct_example_id/..."
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create a Standard Stripe Connect Account

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/standard-accounts

This code demonstrates how to create a new Standard connected account using the Stripe API. It sets the account type to 'standard' and requires your secret key for authentication. While you can prefill additional information, 'type' is the minimum required parameter for account creation.

```curl
curl https://api.stripe.com/v1/accounts \
  -u "<<YOUR_SECRET_KEY>>:" \
  -d type=standard
```

```cli
stripe accounts create  \
  --type=standard
```

```ruby
# Set your secret key. Remember to switch to your live secret key in production.
# See your keys here: https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys
client = Stripe::StripeClient.new("<<YOUR_SECRET_KEY>>")

account = client.v1.accounts.create({type: 'standard'})
```

```python
# Set your secret key. Remember to switch to your live secret key in production.
# See your keys here: https://dashboard.stripe.com/apik eys
client = StripeClient("<<YOUR_SECRET_KEY>>")
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/accounts - Create a connected account

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/api-onboarding

Create an Account where your platform is liable for negative balances, Stripe collects fees from your platform account, and your connected accounts don’t have access to a Stripe-hosted Dashboard. Request any capabilities that your connected accounts need and prefill business type and other information.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/accounts

### Description
Create an Account where your platform is liable for negative balances, Stripe collects fees from your platform account, and your connected accounts don’t have access to a Stripe-hosted Dashboard. Request any capabilities that your connected accounts need. Prefill the business type and any other available information matching your requirements.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/accounts

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **controller** (object) - Required - Configuration for the platform's liabilities, fees, and dashboard access.
  - **losses** (object) - Required - Configuration for platform's liability for losses.
    - **payments** (string) - Required - Specifies who is liable for losses on payments. Example: `application`.
  - **fees** (object) - Required - Configuration for how fees are handled.
    - **payer** (string) - Required - Specifies who pays the fees. Example: `application`.
  - **stripe_dashboard** (object) - Required - Configuration for Stripe Dashboard access.
    - **type** (string) - Required - Specifies the type of Stripe Dashboard access. Example: `none`.
  - **requirement_collection** (string) - Required - Specifies how requirements are collected. Example: `application`.
- **capabilities** (object) - Required - Capabilities requested for the connected account.
  - **card_payments** (object) - Required - Configuration for card payments capability.
    - **requested** (boolean) - Required - Set to `true` to request card payments capability.
  - **transfers** (object) - Required - Configuration for transfers capability.
    - **requested** (boolean) - Required - Set to `true` to request transfers capability.
- **business_type** (string) - Required - The business type of the account. Example: `individual`.
- **country** (string) - Required - The country of the account. Example: `US`.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "controller": {
    "losses": {
      "payments": "application"
    },
    "fees": {
      "payer": "application"
    },
    "stripe_dashboard": {
      "type": "none"
    },
    "requirement_collection": "application"
  },
  "capabilities": {
    "card_payments": {
      "requested": true
    },
    "transfers": {
      "requested": true
    }
  },
  "business_type": "individual",
  "country": "US"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **id** (string) - Unique identifier for the account object.
- **object** (string) - String representing the object’s type. Value is `account`.
- **type** (string) - The type of account. Example: `custom`.
- **business_type** (string) - The business type of the account.
- **capabilities** (object) - Information about the requirements for the account’s capabilities.
- **country** (string) - Two-letter country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "acct_12345Example",
  "object": "account",
  "type": "custom",
  "business_type": "individual",
  "capabilities": {
    "card_payments": {
      "requested": true,
      "status": "active"
    },
    "transfers": {
      "requested": true,
      "status": "active"
    }
  },
  "country": "US",
  "created": 1678886400,
  "livemode": false
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/accounts - Create Connected Account

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/migrate-to-stripe

This endpoint allows you to create a new connected account on Stripe. It's used as part of the API-based onboarding flow for your connected accounts, enabling programmatic registration of new accounts.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/accounts

### Description
Create a new connected account on Stripe. This endpoint is crucial for API-based onboarding workflows, allowing you to programmatically register accounts. You must specify the account `type`, and you can optionally request specific `capabilities` during creation.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/accounts

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **type** (string) - Required - The type of account to create. For standard onboarding, this must be 'standard'.
- **capabilities** (object) - Optional - An object specifying the capabilities to request for the account (e.g., 'card_payments', 'transfers').

### Request Example
```json
{
  "type": "standard"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Details for the success response are not provided in the source text. Refer to the Stripe API documentation for full response details.

#### Response Example
A response example is not provided in the source text.
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/accounts

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/standard-accounts

Use the `/v1/accounts` API to create a new Standard connected account. You can prefill information on the account object, but at a minimum, you must specify the `type` parameter as `standard`.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/accounts

### Description
This endpoint allows you to create a new connected account on Stripe. For Standard accounts, the `type` parameter must be set to `standard`. You can also prefill other account details during creation.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/accounts

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
(None)

#### Query Parameters
(None)

#### Request Body
- **type** (string) - Required - The type of account to create. Must be `standard` for a Standard connected account.
- **capabilities** (object) - Optional - Hash of capabilities the account should have enabled. For example, `{"card_payments": {"requested": true}}`.
- **email** (string) - Optional - The email address of the account holder.
- **country** (string) - Optional - The country in which the account holder resides or is established. Defaults to your platform's country if not provided.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "type": "standard",
  "email": "test@example.com",
  "country": "US"
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Upon successful creation, Stripe returns an Account object.
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the account.
- **object** (string) - Value is `account`.
- **type** (string) - The type of the account, e.g., `standard`.
- **charges_enabled** (boolean) - Whether the account is enabled to receive charges.
- **details_submitted** (boolean) - Whether the account has submitted additional information.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "acct_1G2h3j4k5l6m7n8o",
  "object": "account",
  "capabilities": {
    "card_payments": {
      "status": "inactive"
    },
    "transfers": {
      "status": "inactive"
    }
  },
  "charges_enabled": false,
  "country": "US",
  "created": 1678886400,
  "default_currency": "usd",
  "details_submitted": false,
  "email": "test@example.com",
  "payouts_enabled": false,
  "type": "standard"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Financial Connections Account Data for a Connected Account

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/payouts-bank-accounts

This snippet demonstrates how to retrieve linked Financial Connections Accounts for a connected account. It requires specifying the connected account ID in the Stripe-Account header and using your secret key for authentication. This allows platforms to determine if a user has successfully linked a Financial Connections Account.

```curl
curl -G https://api.stripe.com/v1/financial_connections/accounts \
  -u "<<YOUR_SECRET_KEY>>:" \
  -H "Stripe-Account: {{CONNECTEDACCOUNT_ID}}" \
  -d "account_holder[account]"="{{CONNECTEDACCOUNT_ID}}"
```

```cli
stripe financial_connections accounts list  \
  --stripe-account {{CONNECTEDACCOUNT_ID}} \
  -d "account_holder[account]"="{{CONNECTEDACCOUNT_ID}}"
```

--------------------------------

### List Stripe Financial Connections Accounts for a Connected Account (Java)

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/connect/payouts-bank-accounts_bank-account-collection-integration=prebuilt-web-form

This Java code snippet demonstrates how to retrieve a list of Financial Connections Accounts associated with a specific connected account. It uses the `Stripe.apiKey` for authentication and sets the `Stripe-Account` header to identify the connected account, ensuring the query is scoped correctly. The `AccountListParams` are used to filter by the account holder's connected account ID.

```Java
// Set your secret key. Remember to switch to your live secret key in production.
// See your keys here: https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys
Stripe.apiKey = 
"sk_test_BQokikJOvBiI2HlWgH4olfQ2"
;

AccountListParams params =
  AccountListParams.builder()
    .
setAccountHolder
(
      AccountListParams.AccountHolder.builder()
        .
setAccount
(
"{{CONNECTED_ACCOUNT_ID}}"
)
        .build()
    )
    .build();

RequestOptions requestOptions =
  RequestOptions.builder().setStripeAccount(
"{{CONNECTED_ACCOUNT_ID}}"
).build();

AccountCollection accounts = Account.
list
(params, requestOptions);
```

--------------------------------

### Create Bank Account Token for Connected Account (cURL)

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/issuing/connect/funding

This cURL command creates a bank account token on a connected Stripe account. It requires the connected account ID, bank account details such as country, currency, account holder name, type, routing number, and account number. The generated token can then be used to create a source for debiting the account.

```curl
curl https://api.stripe.com/v1/tokens \
  -u "<<YOUR_SECRET_KEY>>:" \
  -H "Stripe-Account: {{CONNECTEDACCOUNT_ID}}" \
  -d "bank_account[country]"=US \
  -d "bank_account[currency]"=usd \
  -d "bank_account[account_holder_name]"="Jenny Rosen" \
  -d "bank_account[account_holder_type]"=individual \
  -d "bank_account[routing_number]"=110000000 \
  -d "bank_account[account_number]"=000000000009
```

--------------------------------

### POST /v1/accounts

Source: https://docs.stripe.com/financial-accounts/connect/account-management/connected-accounts

Create a new connected account for your platform, requesting necessary capabilities such as 'transfers' and 'treasury' for Financial Accounts. Optionally include 'card_issuing' and 'us_bank_account_ach_payments'.

```APIDOC
## POST /v1/accounts

### Description
This endpoint allows you to create a new connected account on Stripe. It's used to onboard new users or businesses that will interact with your platform via Financial Accounts. You must request capabilities like `transfers` and `treasury` for Financial Accounts functionality.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/v1/accounts

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
_None_

#### Query Parameters
_None_

#### Request Body
- **country** (string) - Required - The country code of the connected account (e.g., 'US').
- **email** (string) - Required - The email address of the account owner.
- **capabilities** (object) - Required - A hash of capabilities to request for the account.
  - **transfers** (object) - Required - Configuration for the `transfers` capability.
    - **requested** (boolean) - Required - Set to `true` to request the transfers capability.
  - **treasury** (object) - Required - Configuration for the `treasury` capability.
    - **requested** (boolean) - Required - Set to `true` to request the treasury capability.
  - **card_issuing** (object) - Optional - Configuration for the `card_issuing` capability, required for issuing cards.
    - **requested** (boolean) - Required - Set to `true` to request the card_issuing capability.
  - **us_bank_account_ach_payments** (object) - Optional - Configuration for the `us_bank_account_ach_payments` capability, required for ACH transfers.
    - **requested** (boolean) - Required - Set to `true` to request the us_bank_account_ach_payments capability.
- **controller** (object) - Optional - Configures the platform's control over the connected account.
  - **dashboard** (object) - Configuration for dashboard access.
    - **type** (string) - Specifies the type of dashboard access (e.g., "none").
  - **losses** (object) - Configures liability for losses.
    - **payments** (string) - Specifies who is responsible for payment losses (e.g., "application").
  - **requirement_collection** (string) - Specifies the method for collecting requirements (e.g., "application").
  - **fees** (object) - Configures fee collection.
    - **payer** (string) - Specifies who pays the fees (e.g., "application").

### Request Example
```json
{
  "country": "US",
  "email": "test@example.com",
  "capabilities": {
    "transfers": {"requested": true},
    "treasury": {"requested": true},
    "card_issuing": {"requested": true},
    "us_bank_account_ach_payments": {"requested": true}
  },
  "controller": {
    "dashboard": {"type": "none"},
    "losses": {"payments": "application"},
    "requirement_collection": "application",
    "fees": {"payer": "application"}
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
A connected account object confirming the created account and its requested capabilities.
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the connected account.
- **object** (string) - Always "account".
- **capabilities** (object) - A hash showing the status of each requested capability.
  - **card_issuing** (string) - The status of the `card_issuing` capability (e.g., "inactive").
  - **treasury** (string) - The status of the `treasury` capability (e.g., "inactive").
  - **us_bank_account_ach_payments** (string) - The status of the `us_bank_account_ach_payments` capability (e.g., "inactive").

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "id": "acct_1234",
  "object": "account",
  "capabilities": {
    "card_issuing": "inactive",
    "treasury": "inactive",
    "us_bank_account_ach_payments": "inactive",
    "transfers": "active"
  },
  "country": "US",
  "email": "test@example.com",
  "type": "custom",
  "created": 1678886400,
  "livemode": false,
  "metadata": {},
  "payouts_enabled": false,
  "charges_enabled": false,
  "details_submitted": false,
  "requirements": {
    "current_deadline": null,
    "currently_due": [],
    "disabled_reason": null,
    "errors": [],
    "eventually_due": [
      "external_account"
    ],
    "past_due": [],
    "pending_verification": []
  }
}
```
```
